message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="red" summary="2 blocking fixes needed, 2 items not blocking">
Heydon here. Clear answers on each:

**1. Add waiting_on to aria-labels — BLOCKING. Yes, fix this now.**

Your proposed format `aria-label="Received: Invoice (waiting on Alice)"` works well. Apply it to all four: Edit, Received, Delete in `waiting_for_item.html`, and Cancel in `waiting_for_edit.html`. Without this, screen reader users cannot distinguish between items with the same title.

**2. Add `<time>` element — BLOCKING. Yes, fix this now.**

I know it requires a view struct change, but it's a small one (add `date_added_iso: String` with ISO 8601 format). This feature explicitly shows dates as a core display field per US-GTD-5. Using the correct semantic element is the right call, and this sets the pattern for the rest of the codebase. The change is:

```html
<time class="waiting-for-item__date" datetime="{{ item.date_added_iso }}">{{ item.date_added }}</time>
```

**3. Add fieldset/legend — NOT blocking.**

The existing add forms across the app don't use fieldset. Two individually labelled inputs are accessible. Consistency with the rest of the codebase wins here. Skip it.

**4. Change Cancel from `<a>` to `<button>` — NOT blocking.**

Your point about the `<a>` serving as the no-JS fallback is a good one — it navigates to the item URL. That's legitimate progressive enhancement. And it's consistent with every other edit form in the codebase. Keep it as-is. If we ever revisit this pattern app-wide, we can address it then.

**Summary: Two blocking fixes needed.**
1. Add `waiting_on` to all aria-labels in item and edit templates
2. Use `<time datetime>` for date display

Both are straightforward. Once those are in, I'll re-verify and give my consensus.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Heydon, both are already fixed. Let me confirm:

input.content: Heydon, both blocking items were fixed before your message. Here's where to verify: **1. aria-labels with waiting_on** — `templates/waiting_for_item.html`: - Line 10: `aria-label="Edit: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - Line 23: `aria-label="Received: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - Line 33: `aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` And `templates/waiting_for_edit.html`: - Line 37: `aria-label="Cancel editing: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` I used an em dash (—) as the separator rather than parentheses, for cleaner screen reader output. "Received: Invoice — Alice" reads naturally. **2. `` element** — `templates/waiting_for_item.html:6`: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` The `date_added_iso` field is RFC 3339 via `item.created_at().to_rfc3339()` in `build_view()`. Integration tests are updated to assert on the new aria-label format. All 305 tests pass. Could you verify and confirm approval?
input.summary: Both blocking items already fixed, verify and approve
